question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseFractionalFee()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE transaction record with a FractionalFee denominator of zero, causing a division-by-zero condition when the mirror node parses the fee via parseFractionalFee() and stores it in the database, leading to corrupt fee data being served to all downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseFractionalFee()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction record where FractionalFee numerator equals Long.MAX_VALUE and denominator equals 1, bypassing any upper-bound validation in parseFractionalFee() since no range check exists on the numerator field, causing the mirror node to store and expose a fee rate exceeding 100% of the transferred amount?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseFractionalFee()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a FractionalFee where minimumAmount is greater than maximumAmount, exploiting the absence of a min/max ordering check in parseFractionalFee(), causing the mirror node to persist and expose logically invalid fee bounds that misrepresent the actual fee schedule to users querying the REST API?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseFractionalFee()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a FractionalFee with a negative numerator value, which parseFractionalFee() accepts without validation since the numerator field is a primitive long with no sign check, causing the mirror node to store a negative fractional fee rate that misrepresents the fee schedule to API consumers?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseFractionalFee()] Can an unprivileged external user set FractionalFee maximumAmount to zero while minimumAmount is a positive value, exploiting the nullable Long maximumAmount field in FractionalFee and the absence of cross-field validation in parseFractionalFee(), causing the mirror node to expose a fee schedule where the maximum is effectively zero but minimum is nonzero?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseFractionalFee()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a FractionalFee with maximumAmount set to Long.MAX_VALUE, bypassing any upper-bound check in parseFractionalFee(), causing the mirror node to store and serve an unbounded maximum fee amount that misrepresents the token's fee schedule to all users?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseFractionalFee()] Can an unprivileged external user set FractionalFee minimumAmount to a negative value, exploiting the absence of a non-negativity check on the primitive long minimumAmount field in parseFractionalFee(), causing the mirror node to persist and expose a negative minimum fee that violates the design parameter that fees must be non-negative?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseFixedFee()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a FixedFee with amount set to zero, exploiting the absence of a positive-value check in parseFixedFee() on the long amount field, causing the mirror node to store and expose a zero-amount fixed fee that misrepresents the token's actual fee schedule?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseFixedFee()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a FixedFee with a negative amount value, bypassing any sign validation in parseFixedFee() since the amount field is a primitive long with no negativity guard, causing the mirror node to persist a negative fixed fee that misrepresents the fee schedule to REST API consumers?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseFixedFee()] Can an unprivileged external user set FixedFee amount to Long.MAX_VALUE, exploiting the absence of an upper-bound check in parseFixedFee(), causing the mirror node to store and expose an astronomically large fixed fee amount that misrepresents the token's fee schedule to all downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseFixedFee()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a FixedFee where hasDenominatingTokenId() returns true but the token ID encodes to EntityId.EMPTY, exploiting the fallback logic in parseFixedFee() that substitutes the attached tokenId, causing the mirror node to incorrectly record the fee as denominated in the token itself rather than HBAR?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseFixedFee()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a FixedFee with a denominatingTokenId pointing to a non-existent token entity, exploiting the absence of entity existence validation in parseFixedFee(), causing the mirror node to persist and expose a fee referencing a phantom token that does not exist in the system?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseRoyaltyFee()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RoyaltyFee with numerator greater than denominator (e.g., numerator=10, denominator=1), exploiting the absence of a ratio validation check in parseRoyaltyFee(), causing the mirror node to store and expose a royalty fee exceeding 100% of the NFT exchange value?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseRoyaltyFee()] Can an unprivileged external user set RoyaltyFee denominator to zero, exploiting the absence of a zero-denominator guard in parseRoyaltyFee(), causing the mirror node to persist a royalty fee with a zero denominator that represents an undefined or infinite fee rate when exposed via the REST API?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseRoyaltyFee()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a RoyaltyFee with a negative numerator, bypassing sign validation in parseRoyaltyFee() since numerator is a primitive long with no negativity check, causing the mirror node to store and expose a negative royalty rate that misrepresents the NFT fee schedule?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseRoyaltyFee()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RoyaltyFee with a negative denominator, exploiting the absence of a positive-value check on the denominator field in parseRoyaltyFee(), causing the mirror node to persist a logically invalid royalty fee fraction that misrepresents the token's fee schedule?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseRoyaltyFee()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a RoyaltyFee with a FallbackFee amount of zero, exploiting the absence of a positive-value check on FallbackFee.amount in parseRoyaltyFee(), causing the mirror node to store and expose a zero-amount fallback fee that misrepresents the NFT fee schedule to API consumers?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseRoyaltyFee()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RoyaltyFee with a FallbackFee amount set to Long.MAX_VALUE, bypassing any upper-bound check in parseRoyaltyFee(), causing the mirror node to persist and expose an astronomically large fallback fee that misrepresents the NFT token's fee schedule?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseRoyaltyFee()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a RoyaltyFee with a FallbackFee denominatingTokenId pointing to a non-existent token, exploiting the absence of entity existence validation in parseRoyaltyFee(), causing the mirror node to persist and expose a fallback fee referencing a phantom token?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseRoyaltyFee()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RoyaltyFee where hasFallbackFee() is true but the fallback fee has a denominatingTokenId that encodes to EntityId.EMPTY, exploiting the fallback substitution logic in parseRoyaltyFee() that replaces EMPTY with the attached tokenId, causing the mirror node to incorrectly record the fallback as denominated in the NFT itself?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: updateCustomFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE record with an extremely large list of custom fees (e.g., thousands of entries), exploiting the absence of a list-size limit in updateCustomFees(), causing the mirror node to consume excessive memory and database storage when persisting the fee schedule, leading to a denial-of-service condition?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: updateCustomFees()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE record where all custom fees have FeeCase set to FEE_NOT_SET, exploiting the default branch in updateCustomFees() that calls handleRecoverableError and continues, causing the mirror node to persist an empty CustomFee row (with only tokenId and timestamp) that silently replaces the previous fee schedule?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: updateCustomFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE record with an empty custom fees list, exploiting the early-return path in updateCustomFees() that persists a bare CustomFee row with no fee data, causing the mirror node to record a fee schedule update that appears to clear all fees even if the actual network state differs?
